Nature: Chiriki is a quiet goddess, but aggressively quiet rather than bashfully. She watches and waits for her moment to act; which means that, while other gods may dabble and interfere more, Chiriki's plans almost always work- and swiftly and efficiently. Associated Major Deity: Earth Mother Relations to other Deities: Origins: Chiriki's ascent is known to have happened (from The Winds). It occurred after the world existed and the Earth Mother had created the plants and beasts upon it, but before there were mortals; meaning she almost certainly predates Helios. Unlike his ascent, The Winds tell us that Chiriki was elevated by the Earth Mother directly due to her predatory prowess; it was a celebration rather than a catastrophe. It's never been clear, but I like to think that, rather than some prosaic hunter like a panther, Chiriki was a goose. It both humbles her in the mind and adds lore to why geese are so very pissy. -Reyvan Activity: Chiriki stays out of mortal affairs for the most part, but will interfere in the natural world to correct an imbalance, remove an unnatural threat, or sometimes to create a new hunting ground by altering the terrain. When she does choose to interact with mortals, it is often to punish rather than to aid. She seems to have no qualms about poor hunters starving, or wild beasts destroying farms and villages. There are a few records, however, of times when mortals living in harmony with the land began to gain magical powers which let them thrive, and also to fend off more powerful and civilized peoples. An exception to this behaviour is, of course, in Thracia, where worship of Chiriki (as Atalanta) is a very civilized and urban affair. For those of us outside that archipelago, it seems that she perhaps tolerates this as a way to learn how to hunt humans- given the tactical and military purposes that the Vestals are put to. The Thracians simply have a different view of her, it seems, noting her keen eye and quick mind, and associating her to the owl, hawk or cat, but without the common hatred of civilization. Aside from any actions she may choose to take, The Huntress is always stalking something, somewhere in the world. I think the only beast she hasn't yet challenged is The Torrasque, and it's doubtful that this exception is from lack of courage. I can not state this with certainty, but a rumour has surfaced that Chiriki's unwillingness to deal with mortals is due to the fact that she was an early patron of the humans who would go on to become the Yuan-Ti. Perhaps she feels her attempt to guide them failed, and has simply moved to easier targets since then. -Reyvan Desired Behaviour in Followers: Piety Benefits: You increase your piety score to Chiriki when you reinforce the goddess’s influence in the world in a concrete way through acts such as these: Hunting a worthy quarry to completion, for food and glory Punishing those who hunt for profit, or who hunt excessively Proving your worth in a contest of hunting skills, such as archery Slaying an aberration, a fiend, or an undead in the name of Chiriki Your piety score to Chiriki decreases if you diminish Chiriki’s influence in the world by encouraging civilization, failing to punish those who exploit nature, or offend her sensibilities through acts such as these: Killing an animal and not making use of it. Dedicating a building or making an animal sacrifice to any god, including Chiriki, except as Atalanta. Protecting a city or farm from natural dangers -Chiriki’s Devotee Piety 3+ Chiriki trait. As a devotee of Chiriki, you have proven yourself a predator of the wilds. You can cast hunter’s mark with this trait a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once) or with spell slots. You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for this spell. -Chiriki’s Votary Piety 10+ Chiriki trait. You can cast speak with animals with this trait a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), or with spell slots. If you can already speak with animals, you instead gain beast bond. You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for this spell. In addition, you have advantage on saving throws against being poisoned and resistance to poison damage. -Chiriki’s Disciple Piety 25+ Chiriki trait. You can now hide when only partially obscured, or , if you already had this ability, you can hide when unobscured as long as you are in a natural environment. You lose the benefit of hiding when you make an attack, dash, or cast a spell requiring verbal components other than hunter's mark, speak with animals or beast bond. -Champion of the Hunt Piety 50+ Chiriki trait. You can increase your Dexterity or Wisdom score by 2 and also increase your maximum for that score by 2.

title: Damu, the Green-VR372
content: A curious question came to me once: if I were to murder Damu, would the world slide slowly into bleak emptiness through attrition, or would it be abrupt due to panic? My best guess is that her work would revert to the Earth Mother, but what if it didn't? I'd need different ploys and games to exploit the existential dread of the two situations. -Reyvan Damu is a Minor God. Regional Names: Demeter (Thracia), The Green, The Wellspring, The Sower, She Who Plants Now and Eats Tomorrow Domains: Life, Nature Regional Worship: Damu is worshiped under many names, in many places, and in ways that mortals don't even understand that they are worshiping her. Nature: Damu is a calm and patient goddess, who understands that all of her work is for the benefit of others. She brings life to plants, to bring life to animals, to bring life to mortals, to bring life to the New Gods. She has never once complained that she can never benefit from this process. Damu is always active in the world, as the work of growth is never-ending. Associated Major Deity: Earth Mother Relations to other Deities: Origins: Damu is probably the first of the Minor Gods, though the actual order between her and The Winds is unknown. Due to age, no record of ascent exists; and given her working it's unlikely that mortals existed on the world at the time. This makes it likely that she was created by the Earth Mother directly, rather than brought to divinity from another state. Activity: Damu works the lands, sowing seeds of every plant and animal, ensuring crops, litters, herds, towns and civilizations replenish themselves in the endless cycle. Desired Behaviour in Followers: Piety Benefits:

title: Ahksha, the Western Wind-VR372
content: There are very few things as depressing as Ahksha. I just can't fathom how a being like him can endure for so long, with so little purpose. -Reyvan Ahksha is a Minor God. Regional Names: Zephyrus (Thracia), The Sad Child, Melancholy, He Who Regrets Domains: Life, Peace Regional Worship: Nature: Manifesting as the smell of rain or a young boy watching a distant storm, Asksha is the calm melancholy of the changing seasons. Generally helpful but not very motivated, he drifts with the currents and clouds. Associated Major Deity: Earth Mother Relations to other Deities: Brother to Douma, the Southern Wind, Faria, the Northern Wind and Estes, the Eastern Wind Origins: An Old God and Child of the Earth Mother, Ahksha has drifted with the seasons since time immemorial. Activity: Generally helpful but not very motivated, he drifts with the currents and clouds. He'll provide any help he can to mortals who can make a good case, but only if it is relatively low effort for him. He doesn't even bother checking to see the outcomes, or if they were lying. Desired Behaviour in Followers: Piety Benefits:

title: Douma, the Southern Wind-VR372
content: Douma is not someone you want to mess with- if you see her coming, you leave. There really is no other option. -Reyvan Douma is a Minor God. Regional Names: Notus (Thracia), Hunger, the Swarm, She Who Consumes Domains: Death, Tempest, War Regional Worship: Nature: Douma is a creature of consumption, desolation and malice. Where Zatar is the tornado, the hurricane, the typhoon; Douma is the desiccating wind that creates the dustbowl and erodes the rock. She roams the world, consuming what is in her path- occasionally she will steer towards a particular direction, which has lead to some cult-like worship. Associated Major Deity: Earth Mother Relations to other Deities: Sister to Ahksha, the Western Wind, Estes, the Eastern Wind and Faria, the Northern Wind Origins: An Old God and Child of the Earth Mother, Douma has plagued the world since time immemorial. Activity: Douma is not mindless, and can be petitioned to avoid an area, or to go to one. Some cults have formed around her, and some mortals, especially agrarian or druidic mortals, provide her offerings in high places to entice her away from their lands. Desired Behaviour in Followers: Piety Benefits:

title: Estes, the Eastern Wind-VR372
content: Estes almost feels like a love child between The Earth Mother and The Radiant Truth- and indeed he might well be. -Reyvan Estes is a Minor God. Regional Names: Eurus (Thracia), The Sky Serpent, Feathered Dragon, Ouroboros, He Who Brings Life Domains: Life, Light, Nature Regional Worship: Estes is worshipped heavily in some civilizations, particularly in places that have smaller settlements and haven't yet found the "truth and art" facets of Helios as valuable as the "please bring a gentle spring with rain and sun so we don't starve". Is often depicted as the lover of Clotho. Nature: A Feathered Serpent, coiling and endless, Estes is the fire writ upon the morning sky and the mist of the high clouds. Estes is not very active, in terms of interfacing with mortals personally. But of all The Winds, he takes his remit the most seriously, and works with diligence to bring fertility and growth to all places, usually in the wake of his siblings. Associated Major Deity: Earth Mother Relations to other Deities: Brother to Ahksha, the Western Wind, Douma, the Southern Wind, and Faria, the Northern Wind Origins: An Old God and Child of the Earth Mother, Estes has graced the world since time immemorial. Activity: Estes travels constantly, renewing the lands of his mother and providing the opportunity for growth. He is somewhat forgotten in "more civilized" lands, but sometimes farmers keep totems, and island folk rarely go a year without celebrating for him. Desired Behaviour in Followers: Piety Benefits:

title: Faria, the Northern Wind-VR372
content: Faria is a strong example of what a god should be, in my opinion; she is engaged in the mortal world, opinionated, capricious, merciless and just free enough with her power that mortals have a positive opinion of her- so they keep coming back for more. -Reyvan Faria is a Minor God. Regional Names: Boreas (Thracia), The Giantess, She Who Usurps Domains: Tempest, Trickery Regional Worship: Some veneration among giants, but not worship. Nature: Faria has a whimsical nature, endless patience for activities and people she enjoys, and an implacable thirst for vengeance- and also a strongly cruel sense of humour. The most active of The Winds, Faria will interface with mortals fairly regularly in her usual Storm Giant form; given how few records describe this, it's likely that they never knew she was a Goddess. Associated Major Deity: Earth Mother Relations to other Deities: Sister to Ahksha, the Western Wind, Douma, the Southern Wind, and Estes, the Eastern Wind. Origins: An Old God and Child of the Earth Mother, Faria has roamed the world since time immemorial. Activity: Faria engages in what she describes as "projects", which can vary in scope and scale wildly. Helping a settlement be established, removing a problematic mountain, restoring a rightful heir, or torturing someone she believes deserves it- being so old, her patience means that she will pursue her chosen goal relentlessly for centuries if required. Desired Behaviour in Followers: Piety Benefits:

title: The Winds-VR372
content: The Winds are Minor Gods and Aspects of the Earth Mother. Ahksha, the Western Wind Douma, the Southern Wind Estes, the Eastern Wind Faria, the Northern Wind

title: Dun, the Smelter-VR372
content: Dun is the most straightforward being in terms of his mind, and the most convoluted being in terms of his physicality. He is a tool, but a pan-dimensional one. -Reyvan Dun is a Minor God. Regional Names: Furnace Master, the Crucible (dwarven cultural), He Who Prepares Domains: Forge, Order, War. Regional Worship: Nature: Dun is a craftsman, or more accurately a crafting tool, of prodigious skill and productivity; but lacking almost entirely in creativity. It is said that Volkanus removed his own patience to craft Dun, and is thought to be the prototype of what eventually became the dwarven people. Dun provides Volkanus with the base materials for his crafting, and occasionally crafts simple tools of legendary quality on his own. Dun unfolds copies of himself in a constant growing and shrinking fractal progression to travel both within and between planes; mortals who have seen this have occasionally been driven mad. Dun does whatever his Master commands, and so is generally accepted by mortals as his representative. He is always in the mortal world; if you need to find him, start by visiting the great dwarven strongholds, as he is likely at one of them. Associated Major Deity: Volkanus the Maker and Reshaper of all Things Relations to other Deities: Dun doesn't exactly have opinions, and so it's difficult to say that he has relations to anyone but his Master. Origins: Crafted in the original Volcano in Thracia by Volkanus shortly after his Ascension. Activity: Constantly working, making and providing, Dun is usually in a dwarven metropolis or stronghold, but always at a forge or smeltery. Although he can't generate ideas, the things he has made are usually taken as exemplars for dwarven master smiths to copy. Desired Behaviour in Followers: Piety Benefits:

title: Lady Racca, the Planner-VR372
content: The influence of Lady Racca is responsible for most of the beauty that the disciples of Helios take credit for. -Reyvan Lady Racca is a Minor God. Regional Names: St. Aeonath (The Shining Vale 35-current), Berronar Truesilver (dwarven cultural), She Who Thinks and Makes Domains: Order, Peace, War Regional Worship: Worship of Lady Racca is often based in construction guilds in human civilization, and the reason for that construction is usually growth; so worship alongside Parvati is quite common. When Aeonath was sanctified after his death, the Cathedral in Haut-Revanne was already under construction. Immediately dedicated to him, the city, Duchy Revanne, and The Shining Vale saw a rampant increase in growth and construction. Nature: Goddess of cities and city-building, Lady Racca is usually depicted standing tall with a compass (the drawing kind) and a ruler. This holds true in The Shining Vale, except it is a depiction of Aeonath and he is always backlit with golden light. Associated Major Deity: Volkanus the Maker and Reshaper of all Things Relations to other Deities: Origins: To aid with the reconstruction of Thracia after Volkanus' ascent, he removed his sense of proportion- and from it crafted Lady Racca. Activity: Lady Racca operates independently from Volkanus and serves as one of his advisors for his projects; when he chooses to listen to her. When dealing with mortals, she will often take the form of a dwarven master mason, architect or planner. She gives excellent advice, and is secretly the mind behind many of the architectural marvels of the world, thought most of them have been lost to time. Desired Behaviour in Followers: Piety Benefits:

title: Fantis, the Weaver of Beads-VR372
content: If you ever thought that you've been bedeviled, perhaps literally, or that a demon has possessed you to do terrible things you regret, again- possibly literally, you can thank Fantis. Don't blame us. -Reyvan Fantis is a Minor God. Regional Names: The Spider, Bringer of Sorrow, She Who Places Burdens Onto Our Lives Domains: Knowledge, Order Regional Worship: Fantis is more appeased than worshipped by mortals. They want to keep her ministrations far, far away. Fantis does, however, provide services for Oracles. Nature: A crystalline glass woman, giant in size, with spider-like limbs protruding from her back and over her shoulders, Fantis is an imposing figure. She is however, relatively non-threatening unless provoked, and has a calm, logical demeanor. Fantis works tirelessly at her table, pulling threads from the weave of fate out of the air and placing weights shaped like slotted disks upon them. Thus spread the burdens of the living. Associated Major Deity: Lachesis Relations to other Deities: Paratiritis is a trusted friend and useful compatriot for Fantis. Because Jaraxx tried to use knowledge of the weave of fate to his advantage, Fantis has refused to reduce his burdens. Whether she added them originally isn't known, but seems likely. Origins: Given the manner of ascent of Lachesis, it's likely that Fantis was created or born to serve a specific need or change at some point. We can't know what that was, or if it is still relevant, but she serves her Mistress well and so continues to exist. Activity: Fantis can't spare time to leave her table, but she can move it. She has been found in many places over time; usually in places of oracular prophecy. At last notice, she was in the Great Temple in the Sky where the Obelisk of Divination can be found. Angering or slighting Fantis or Lachesis usually ends up with more weights set on your thread. Those who find ways to add those weights themselves (to their own thread or to others') quickly become the focus of Fantis' ire. Desired Behaviour in Followers: Piety Benefits:

title: Lady Saoirse, the Midwife-VR372
content: The world would be much less crowded without the intervention of Lady Saoirse, and there would be many more desperate soon-to-be parents. I suppose it comes out a wash. -Reyvan Lady Saoirse is a Minor God. Regional Names: St. Elyse (Duchy Revanne, Lausanne), Lady Saoirse (Duchy Corwyn), She Who Brings New Life Domains: Life, Peace Regional Worship: Nature: Lady Saoirse is depicted with cradled arms and a smile, carrying no articles of faith. She presides over childbirth and midwifery, leading to much faster population gains and much smaller families than if her influence didn't exist. Associated Major Deity: Clotho Relations to other Deities: Origins: Lady Saoirse may have existed before, but she was first noticed by the pantheon at large quite recently- at the birth of Jaraxx, the Rot; having drawn him from a pile of corpses and cleaned him up, she then moved on to other duties with very little comment. Activity: Lady Saoirse is always quietly working, teaching midwives, and fulfilling her calling with no fuss, muss or bother. She only rears her head up when something she loves is threatened- she is a mother to so many, after all. Desired Behaviour in Followers: Piety Benefits:

title: Auguri, the Mage-VR372
content: A very useful source of knowledge, with a very inconvenient attitude towards interference on the mortal plane. I think we could be allies, if uneasy ones, but I am not willing to sacrifice the chance for it until he emerges from his hiding place. -Reyvan Auguri is a Minor God. Regional Names: Chronos (Thracia), Adoric (The Shining Vale), Fillain Entwistle (Sendria), Herr Doktor Ivo Tollstrup (Hellenburg) Domains: Arcana, Forge, Knowledge, Trickery Regional Worship: None directly, see activities. Nature: Auguri is fascinated by the progression of mortals, particularly humans, when it comes to their overreach with magic. His past history has informed his opinion that wizards should co-operate rather than compete. Auguri appears usually as a human man in his early 20s with wavy blond hair, wearing fine but not ostentatious clothes. Associated Major Deity: None Relations to other Deities: My divinations indicate that Auguri is afraid of Zatar, which has lead to his current activities and his decision to hide. Origins: Auguri was born Caleb Druth, a minor noble in the Cimmerian Empire. Learning magic from a young age, he became a prodigious inventor of new spells- and indeed a new explorative field of magic, Chronomancy. When Quidun fell and Isthariel ascended to power, Caleb began intense study on how best to protect himself from political movements like this- his conclusion being, in the end, that if they could be foreseen and/or made as if they never happened, the outcome would be better than if they were punished or blocked. His work towards this goal proceeded until, after the period of Isthariel's exile, she returned as Zatar. Caleb's preparations interfaced strangely with the divine magic surrounding the newly born Goddess of Carnage, and instead of reversing the causal events it pulled Caleb into them. Drawing a portion of the power of Zatar's ascent, Caleb became Auguri, the Mage. He attempted to use his new powers to stop the Fall of Cimmeria, but was simply overpowered; once again his preparations saved him, preventing his destruction or punishment at the hands of Zatar. I have often wondered if this "Caleb Druth" is the same person as Hazidkal, given the ability to play with the flow of time. If true, that means that two gods who are the same being are here simultaneously, which may provide opportunities to exploit certain paradoxes. -Reyvan Activity: Auguri has set up towers in many places across the world's surface and underworld. These towers exist in fixed locations, but span vast lengths of time- themselves existing at all moments across that timespan simultaneously. They are not difficult to enter, but generally require some simple item or token to prevent someone stumbling in, and individuals are only allowed to enter once in their life, to avoid the paradox of entering the same extra-temporal space, in the same location, simultaneously. He invites aspiring wizards in, and trains them for years or decades. His magic is such that they can be returned to the entry point at any point in the timeline they choose; but being clever enough to not anger The Fates, Auguri abides by the rules of causality with strict attention and teaches his students to do the same. In the rare event he finds a guest that he trusts with this responsibility, he may make an exception and allow them to return before they left; instructing them to make no changes to events that they know to have happened. In his towers, and for the same reason, guests are kept segregated so none may inform another of what they know of history- for one's history could easily be another's future, and then they are bound to that causal chain. Auguri's interventions, under many aliases, are known to have resulted in the creation of the following arcane organizations: The Magesterium Panopticon The Order of Yellow The Thracian Anti-Witchcraft Alliance C.A.L.I.S.T.O This list, which I can not ever enumerate as it may grow or shrink over time depending on what will have been done in the future-present, is presumed to be complete. I gave this a great deal of thought, and I have accepted that I have limitations in this regard. -Reyvan These organizations each, in their own way, do serve to prevent the internecine magical warfare that Auguri was afraid of; but once inevitably taken over by mortals, some have twisted their original purpose and become magocratic political powers, sometimes competing with each other in the very way he was trying to avoid. Being in hiding as he is, he has not had the opportunity to make any further corrections. Desired Behaviour in Followers: Piety Benefits: Clerics of Auguri may use Intelligence as their Cleric spellcasting ability, and/or Wisdom for Wizard spellcasting if they take both classes. Auguri can teach many spells and powers not normally available to players.

title: Jaraxx, the Rot-VR372
content: Quite a gentleman, in fact. He tends a good garden- you should go see it sometime. -Reyvan Jaraxx is a Minor God. Regional Names: King of Spores, He Who Spreads Through Others Domains: Death, Life, Nature, Order Regional Worship: Although Jaraxx has had cults in the past, since his entrapment in the events following the ascent of Zatar he has found few who would pay him homage. Nature: A tall and spindly fungal creature, Jaraxx is a creature devoted to order- his order. The controlled progression from life, to death, to decomposition and then to reanimation is his primary concern; he tries to control the process by domineering the surrounding plant and animal life, including mortals. Associated Major Deity: None. Relations to other Deities: Though Fantis is angry with Jaraxx for trying to bend fate using preknowledge, he holds no grudge. Although he is nowhere near powerful enough, Jaraxx still harbours plans to destroy Zatar someday. Jaraxx is entrapped by the recuperating Anuket, but neither are aware of this situation. Origins: Jaraxx was pulled from a pile of rotting corpses by Lady Saoirse before the ascent of Quidun, which was stopped. He became active almost immediately to prevent the Fall of Cimmeria, but was unsuccessful. He is the only known Minor God to come from the aborted ascent of Quidun, so either it was stopped early, or we aren't yet aware of them. Activity: For nearly all of his existence, Jaraxx has lived on a small island in the archipelago of the bay formed during the Fall of Cimmeria. Marooned there by Zatar and unable to cross the water of the bay as Thalassus has declared it to be sacrosanct as the recovery place for Anuket, Jaraxx has found himself with with an inability to cross water. He has often tried to bargain his way ashore with mortals on their vessels; some have died trying to double cross him, some have refused and been slain on the spot, but most have simply backed away from the situation. He is unsettling, after all. Desired Behaviour in Followers: Piety Benefits:

title: Minor Gods-VR372
content: Aesclepius, the Mender Aesthenes, the Glorious Aethenius, the Scholar Ahksha, the Western Wind Anuket, the Flowing Water Auguri, the Mage Chiriki, the Huntress Damu, the Green Douma, the Southern Wind Dun, the Smelter Estes, the Eastern Wind Fantis, the Weaver of Beads Faria, the Northern Wind Frouros, the Watchman Jaraxx, the Rot Kor, the Tireless Lady Racca, the Planner Lady Saoirse, the Midwife Paratiritis, the Seeing Parvati, the Accepting Themis, the Righteous Trikar, the Shrike

title: Battlesworn-VR372
content: In Thracia the Battlesworn believe that if war was ever to cease, Maahes (or Polemos, as they know him) would die; and when war broke out again a new God of War would emerge, causing massive destructions due to their Ascension and then most certainly being less interested in honor than blood-thirst. It is for this reason that they encourage constant war and competition between the Kingdoms, and their doctrine and traditional position of power have been persuasive enough to gain acceptance from the people and tolerance from the other faiths.

title: Nightwatchers-VR372
content: Followers of Astere of the Night Sky in Thracia, the Nightwatchers serve as guardians against the things that lurk in the darkness.

title: Piety - Optional Rule-VR372
content: Piety is an Optional Rule from the Mythic Odysseys of Theros sourcebook for 5th Edition Dungeons and Dragons, published in 2020. Some of the Deities presented in this text are adapted from Deities found in that book, but made more suitable to the world of Irithiel; but most of the Deities have been created herein, drawing of course on the mythology of cultures found in the original and most popular campaign setting, Terra.
